content:textarea:6:3.name: Paragraph
content:textarea:6:3.html: The Salvation Army responds to those in need in times of crisis and disaster.
content:textarea:6:3.content: The Salvation Army responds to those in need in times of crisis and disaster.
content:textarea:6:3.newLoc: design:columns:1 column - 1 row my-3:54:20-1:1 column - 1 row:0

content:text:7:86.name: Heading
content:text:7:86.newLoc: design:columns:1 column - 1 row my-3:54:20-1:1 column - 1 row:0

content:textarea:8:1.name: Paragraph
content:textarea:8:1.html: In April of 2020, The Salvation Army of Oconee County responded to a powerful F3 tornado that blew through the county resulting in one fatality and causing millions in damages to approximately 1600 homes, nearly 650 of which were declared destroyed or condemned.
content:textarea:8:1.content: In April of 2020, The Salvation Army of Oconee County responded to a powerful F3 tornado that blew through the county resulting in one fatality and causing millions in damages to approximately 1600 homes, nearly 650 of which were declared destroyed or condemned.
content:textarea:8:1.newLoc: design:columns:1 column - 1 row my-3:54:20-1:1 column - 1 row:0

content:textarea:9:94.name: Paragraph
content:textarea:9:94.newLoc: design:columns:1 column - 1 row my-3:54:20-1:1 column - 1 row:0

content:textarea:10:10.name: Paragraph
content:textarea:10:10.html: The Salvation Army initially responded for weeks by serving meals, helping to coordinate housing, providing clothing, food, water and other life sustaining assistance to nearly 4,500 residents.
content:textarea:10:10.content: The Salvation Army initially responded for weeks by serving meals, helping to coordinate housing, providing clothing, food, water and other life sustaining assistance to nearly 4,500 residents.
content:textarea:10:10.newLoc: design:columns:1 column - 1 row my-3:54:20-1:1 column - 1 row:0

content:textarea:11:50.name: Paragraph
content:textarea:11:50.html: For several months after, The Salvation Army continued to assist residents with a variety of needs. Today The Salvation Army of Oconee continues to serve on the Oconee County Long Term Recovery Group and the multi-county Upstate VOAD to help with local disasters and to plan and prepare for county response services for future events.
content:textarea:11:50.content: For several months after, The Salvation Army continued to assist residents with a variety of needs. Today The Salvation Army of Oconee continues to serve on the Oconee County Long Term Recovery Group and the multi-county Upstate VOAD to help with local disasters and to plan and prepare for county response services for future events.
content:textarea:11:50.newLoc: design:columns:1 column - 1 row my-3:54:20-1:1 column - 1 row:0

content:textarea:12:48.name: Paragraph
content:textarea:12:48.html: The Salvation Army of Oconee County also serves as a member of Oconee County’s Emergency Operations Center (EOC) as they implement the Oconee Nuclear Station drills each year.
content:textarea:12:48.content: The Salvation Army of Oconee County also serves as a member of Oconee County’s Emergency Operations Center (EOC) as they implement the Oconee Nuclear Station drills each year.
content:textarea:12:48.newLoc: design:columns:1 column - 1 row my-3:54:20-1:1 column - 1 row:0

content:textarea:22:68.name: Paragraph
content:textarea:22:68.html: While each disaster creates its own unique circumstances and special needs, Salvation Army disaster relief efforts focus on seven core services. These services may be modified based on the magnitude of the disaster and adapted to meet the specific needs of individual survivors.
content:textarea:22:68.content: While each disaster creates its own unique circumstances and special needs, Salvation Army disaster relief efforts focus on seven core services. These services may be modified based on the magnitude of the disaster and adapted to meet the specific needs of individual survivors.
content:textarea:22:68.newLoc: design:columns:1 column - 1 row my-3:54:20-1:1 column - 1 row:0

content:wysiwyg_basic:23:13.name: WYSIWYG
content:wysiwyg_basic:23:13.html:
Training

The first step in being ready to respond to an emergency is training. In partnership with other agencies, The Salvation Army’s disaster training program offers a variety of courses designed to help individuals and communities prepare for emergency events and become trained disaster volunteers.
content:wysiwyg_basic:23:13.content:
Training

The first step in being ready to respond to an emergency is training. In partnership with other agencies, The Salvation Army’s disaster training program offers a variety of courses designed to help individuals and communities prepare for emergency events and become trained disaster volunteers.

content:wysiwyg_basic:25:39.name: WYSIWYG
content:wysiwyg_basic:25:39.html:
Donations Management

The Salvation Army is one of the nation’s leaders in responsibly collecting, sorting, and distributing donated goods. The Salvation Army encourages cash donations as the best and most flexible way to help and solicits only those in-kind donations which can be effectively received and efficiently distributed.
content:wysiwyg_basic:25:39.content:
Donations Management

The Salvation Army is one of the nation’s leaders in responsibly collecting, sorting, and distributing donated goods. The Salvation Army encourages cash donations as the best and most flexible way to help and solicits only those in-kind donations which can be effectively received and efficiently distributed.

content:wysiwyg_basic:27:70.name: WYSIWYG
content:wysiwyg_basic:27:70.html:
Disaster Social Services

After a family has lost everything in a disaster, The Salvation Army is there to provide emergency assistance to help meet survivors’ most urgent needs for food, clothing, shelter, and medical services.
content:wysiwyg_basic:27:70.content:
Disaster Social Services

After a family has lost everything in a disaster, The Salvation Army is there to provide emergency assistance to help meet survivors’ most urgent needs for food, clothing, shelter, and medical services.

content:wysiwyg_basic:30:29.name: WYSIWYG
content:wysiwyg_basic:30:29.html:
Emergency Communications

The Salvation Army Team Emergency Radio Network (SATERN) includes a worldwide network of volunteer amateur radio operators and other communications specialists, who may be mobilized to transmit emergency information during a disaster event.
content:wysiwyg_basic:30:29.content:
Emergency Communications

The Salvation Army Team Emergency Radio Network (SATERN) includes a worldwide network of volunteer amateur radio operators and other communications specialists, who may be mobilized to transmit emergency information during a disaster event.

content:wysiwyg_basic:32:39.name: WYSIWYG
content:wysiwyg_basic:32:39.html:
Emotional & Spiritual Care

Motivated by the Christian faith, The Salvation Army deploys specially trained individuals to offer emotional and spiritual care to rescue workers and disaster survivors.
content:wysiwyg_basic:32:39.content:
Emotional & Spiritual Care

Motivated by the Christian faith, The Salvation Army deploys specially trained individuals to offer emotional and spiritual care to rescue workers and disaster survivors.

content:wysiwyg_basic:34:67.name: WYSIWYG
content:wysiwyg_basic:34:67.html:
Food Service

When disaster strikes, one of the first signs that help is on the way is often the arrival of a Salvation Army mobile feeding unit, offering
meals, snacks, and drinks to rescue workers and survivors.
content:wysiwyg_basic:34:67.content:
Food Service

When disaster strikes, one of the first signs that help is on the way is often the arrival of a Salvation Army mobile feeding unit, offering
meals, snacks, and drinks to rescue workers and survivors.

content:wysiwyg_basic:36:82.name: WYSIWYG
content:wysiwyg_basic:36:82.html:
Recovery

The Salvation Army supports long-term disaster recovery operations with flexible programming that is adaptable to the unique needs of individual communities.
content:wysiwyg_basic:36:82.content:
Recovery

The Salvation Army supports long-term disaster recovery operations with flexible programming that is adaptable to the unique needs of individual communities.

content:text:39:30.name: Heading
content:text:39:30.content: How Salvation Army’s Disaster Services Began
content:text:39:30.newLoc: design:columns:1 column - 1 row my-3:54:20-1:1 column - 1 row:0

content:textarea:40:40.name: Paragraph
content:textarea:40:40.html: On September 8, 1900, a hurricane devastated Galveston, Texas leaving more than 5,000 people dead. The city, sustaining extensive property damage, was virtually destroyed. While survivors were still reeling from this onslaught of nature, Salvation Army National Commander Frederick Booth-Tucker ordered Salvation Army officers from across America to proceed to the disaster site to provide spiritual assistance. The Salvation Army's first national disaster appeal for funds was generously supported by caring Americans.
content:textarea:40:40.content: On September 8, 1900, a hurricane devastated Galveston, Texas leaving more than 5,000 people dead. The city, sustaining extensive property damage, was virtually destroyed. While survivors were still reeling from this onslaught of nature, Salvation Army National Commander Frederick Booth-Tucker ordered Salvation Army officers from across America to proceed to the disaster site to provide spiritual assistance. The Salvation Army's first national disaster appeal for funds was generously supported by caring Americans.
content:textarea:40:40.newLoc: design:columns:1 column - 1 row my-3:54:20-1:1 column - 1 row:0

content:textarea:41:75.name: Paragraph
content:textarea:41:75.html: From these beginnings, The Salvation Army has developed local, regional, and national disaster services programs. In North and South Carolina, The Salvation Army remains ready for any disaster that may occur. There are hundreds of Salvation Army personnel on call from all of our state's regions to quickly deploy to provide emergency food, shelter, and spiritual needs. Routinely, The Salvation Army's units deploy to provide for the practical and spiritual needs of first responders.
content:textarea:41:75.content: From these beginnings, The Salvation Army has developed local, regional, and national disaster services programs. In North and South Carolina, The Salvation Army remains ready for any disaster that may occur. There are hundreds of Salvation Army personnel on call from all of our state's regions to quickly deploy to provide emergency food, shelter, and spiritual needs. Routinely, The Salvation Army's units deploy to provide for the practical and spiritual needs of first responders.
content:textarea:41:75.newLoc: design:columns:1 column - 1 row my-3:54:20-1:1 column - 1 row:0

content:textarea:42:55.name: Paragraph
content:textarea:42:55.html: From destructive weather to state and national tragedies, The Salvation Army is ready and willing to assist with a formidable group of personnel and stock of emergency supplies. The red shield continues to be a beacon of compassion in the Carolinas. It is immediate aid, psychological support, and spiritual counsel to individuals and families whose lives have been disrupted or shattered by forces beyond their control.
content:textarea:42:55.content: From destructive weather to state and national tragedies, The Salvation Army is ready and willing to assist with a formidable group of personnel and stock of emergency supplies. The red shield continues to be a beacon of compassion in the Carolinas. It is immediate aid, psychological support, and spiritual counsel to individuals and families whose lives have been disrupted or shattered by forces beyond their control.
content:textarea:42:55.newLoc: design:columns:1 column - 1 row my-3:54:20-1:1 column - 1 row:0

content:text:43:29.name: Heading
content:text:43:29.content: Learn More about Emergency Disaster Services
content:text:43:29.newLoc: design:columns:1 column - 1 row my-3:54:20-1:1 column - 1 row:0

content:textarea:44:94.name: Paragraph
content:textarea:44:94.html: The Salvation Army USA has a website dedicated to our work in response to disasters. The site is updated regularly with news, stats, and information and is a great resource, particularly in times of disaster response. It also has information about how you can get involved as a trained volunteer with us in order to help support the work of The Salvation Army in response to emergency and disaster situations.
content:textarea:44:94.content: The Salvation Army USA has a website dedicated to our work in response to disasters. The site is updated regularly with news, stats, and information and is a great resource, particularly in times of disaster response. It also has information about how you can get involved as a trained volunteer with us in order to help support the work of The Salvation Army in response to emergency and disaster situations.
content:textarea:44:94.newLoc: design:columns:1 column - 1 row my-3:54:20-1:1 column - 1 row:0
